Q01-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你能不能马上抓住 thesis 的主轴。
Q01-main.conclusion: My thesis develops and validates a post-pandemic evaluation system for age-friendly urban communities in Henan.
Q01-main.logic: It starts from the gap of lacking one local and systematic tool, then builds a full method chain from indicator extraction to validation.
Q01-main.evidence: The study moved from 410 texts to 847 candidates, then 55 indicators, and finally 48 validated indicators.
Q01-main.boundary: This result validates the framework, but it does not claim to represent every community in Henan.

Q02-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你的选题是不是有研究价值，不只是个人兴趣。
Q02-main.conclusion: I chose this topic because aging and post-pandemic risk made age-friendly community evaluation a real and urgent problem in Henan.
Q02-main.logic: Existing frameworks are not local enough, not systematic enough, and not strong enough in health and emergency needs.
Q02-main.evidence: My framework later showed that health services and emergency response together account for 39.77 percent of the total weight.
Q02-main.boundary: So the topic is not only practical. It also fills a clear research gap.

Q03-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你能不能守住“空间”这个题目，不被说成跑题。
Q03-main.conclusion: My study is still about community space, but I evaluate it from a space performance view rather than a pure form view.
Q03-main.logic: For older adults, space works only when health support, emergency response, and community support also help the space function well.
Q03-main.evidence: That is why the final framework includes health, emergency, transport, and support dimensions, not only physical design.
Q03-main.boundary: I am not saying space is everything. I am saying these factors directly affect how space is used.

Q04-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你能不能把方法链讲清楚，并说明为什么不是普通 Delphi-AHP。
Q04-main.conclusion: My method is a data-driven chain: SciBERT for extraction, clustering for structure, Fuzzy Delphi for validation, AHP for weighting, and case study for testing.
Q04-main.logic: The key difference is that I did not begin with a researcher-made list. I began with text-based indicator extraction.
Q04-main.evidence: The chain moved from 410 texts to 48 final indicators and then to three case-study scores.
Q04-main.boundary: This reduces subjectivity at the front end, but it does not remove subjectivity completely.

Q05-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你的理论基础是不是清楚、是不是能解释结果。
Q05-main.conclusion: The main theory is person-environment fit, supported by other frameworks such as WHO age-friendly community and resilience thinking.
Q05-main.logic: This theory explains why the framework must include not only physical space, but also health support, safety, and social support.
Q05-main.evidence: The weighted results later showed that health services, emergency response, and transport became the top three domains.
Q05-main.boundary: So WHO is an important reference, but not the single core theory of the thesis.

Q06-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你的研究问题、目标和结果是不是一条线，而不是散的。
Q06-main.conclusion: Yes, the research questions, objectives, methods, and results match each other clearly.
Q06-main.logic: Objective one identifies factors, objective two ranks them, and objective three develops and validates the system.
Q06-main.evidence: This is shown by the chain 410 texts to 55 indicators, then 48 final indicators, and then validation in three communities.
Q06-main.boundary: The weakest part is external generalization, but the framework validation itself was achieved.

Q07-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你的语料来源是不是透明、可复现、站得住。
Q07-main.conclusion: The 410 texts were selected through a systematic screening process based on relevance, quality, authority, and timeliness.
Q07-main.logic: I did not rely on one source only, because a practical framework needs theory, policy, and real standards together.
Q07-main.evidence: The corpus included 345 academic papers, 31 industry survey reports, and 34 standard documents.
Q07-main.boundary: So the goal was not to collect every text, but to build a reliable and balanced evidence base.

Q08-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你为什么选 SciBERT，而不是为了显得高级。
Q08-main.conclusion: I chose SciBERT because it is designed for scientific text and fits my literature-based indicator extraction task better than a general model.
Q08-main.logic: My study used papers, reports, and standards, so I needed a model that handles academic language and domain terms well.
Q08-main.evidence: In my study, SciBERT reached 84.8 percent accuracy and a weighted F1 score of 0.848.
Q08-main.boundary: SciBERT was not the final judge. Its results were still checked by experts and later validation.

Q09-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你 847 到 55 的过程是不是透明，不是随意砍指标。
Q09-main.conclusion: The reduction from 847 to 55 was a structured filtering process, not an arbitrary cut.
Q09-main.logic: I removed noise, overlap, weak relevance, and low operability step by step so that only strong and usable indicators remained.
Q09-main.evidence: After that filtering, 55 indicators went into expert validation, and 48 were finally retained.
Q09-main.boundary: Some judgment is still needed in filtering, so I controlled it with clear rules and later expert review.

Q10-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你的聚类选择是不是和后面框架结构匹配。
Q10-main.conclusion: I chose hierarchical clustering because I needed a layered framework, not just flat groups.
Q10-main.logic: The study had to move from a list of indicators to a meaningful multi-level structure for later weighting.
Q10-main.evidence: The final framework was organized into 8 primary domains, 14 secondary elements, and 48 tertiary indicators.
Q10-main.boundary: So the choice was driven by structure and interpretability, not by popularity alone.

Q11-main.purposeZh: 老师想看专家数据是不是可靠，不是只靠“专家说了算”。
Q11-main.conclusion: I validated the expert data through a two-round Fuzzy Delphi process and several reliability checks.
Q11-main.logic: The goal was to test consensus, stability, and internal reliability, not just collect one round of opinions.
Q11-main.evidence: The study used 15 experts, two rounds, d-value improvement from 0.087 to 0.076, Cronbach's alpha above 0.88, and ICC above 0.85.
Q11-main.boundary: So the expert stage is still expert-based, but it is structured and testable rather than informal.

Q12-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你怎么把软指标做得尽量客观和可比。
Q12-main.conclusion: Service and satisfaction indicators were measured through multi-source evidence and then standardized into one scoring system.
Q12-main.logic: I did not treat them as pure impressions. I linked them to records, feedback, coverage, field checks, and comparable scoring rules.
Q12-main.evidence: The case stage combined field observation, service records, administrative data, and available feedback before weighting the final scores.
Q12-main.boundary: Some subjectivity remains in these indicators, but it was controlled through triangulation and standardization.

Q13-main.purposeZh: 老师想看案例评估是不是规范，尤其会追你‘到底几个人评’这种细节。
Q13-main.conclusion: The case evaluation followed a standard protocol, and I must report the exact evaluator number from my original records in a consistent way.
Q13-main.logic: The key point is that reliability comes from a stable protocol, multi-source evidence, and clear scoring rules, not from vague wording.
Q13-main.evidence: All three communities were assessed under the same 48-indicator framework and the same weighted scoring logic.
Q13-main.boundary: If the number is described differently in different places, I need to unify that before the viva.

Q14-main.purposeZh: 老师想看三个社区的数据来源是不是扎实，而不是随手拿来的。
Q14-main.conclusion: The three community datasets came from triangulated sources rather than from one single channel.
Q14-main.logic: I used field observation, measurement, community records, service data, and supporting location-based information to build the case data.
Q14-main.evidence: All data were entered into the same 48-indicator framework and produced three differentiated total scores.
Q14-main.boundary: The data richness was not fully identical across communities, so standardization and quality control were necessary.

Q15-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你能不能承认 3 个案例的限制，同时守住验证价值。
Q15-main.conclusion: Three communities are not enough for statistical generalization, but they are enough for framework validation.
Q15-main.logic: The aim was to test whether the system can distinguish different community types and show practical discrimination in real cases.
Q15-main.evidence: The three scores were 85.27, 71.24, and 54.50, which show clear separation across the cases.
Q15-main.boundary: So I claim validation and discriminatory power, not province-wide representativeness.

Q16-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你能不能把‘做完 3 个社区以后到底证明了什么’说清楚。
Q16-main.conclusion: The three-community validation shows that the framework has practical use and clear discriminatory power.
Q16-main.logic: If the system can separate different types of communities and explain their strengths and weaknesses, then it is working as an evaluation tool.
Q16-main.evidence: The final scores were 85.27 for Provence, 71.24 for Fenghuangcheng, and 54.50 for Wei'erlu, with a range of 30.77.
Q16-main.boundary: This proves usability and structure, but it does not yet prove universal generalization.

Q17-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你知不知道自己论文最核心的结果证据在哪。
Q17-main.conclusion: The core result is the weighted structure of the framework together with the three-community validation result.
Q17-main.logic: The weights show what matters most in theory, and the case scores show that the framework works in practice.
Q17-main.evidence: Health services, emergency response, and transport ranked as the top three domains, and the case scores clearly separated the three communities.
Q17-main.boundary: If I have to choose only one result table, I would choose the three-community score comparison because it proves the tool can work.

Q18-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你能不能把核心发现说成一句真发现，而不是泛泛而谈。
Q18-main.conclusion: My core finding is that post-pandemic age-friendly communities must be judged not only by accessibility, but also by health support, emergency response, and mobility.
Q18-main.logic: The post-pandemic context changed the priority structure, so the center of the framework moved beyond pure physical design.
Q18-main.evidence: Health services ranked first at 23.01 percent, emergency response second at 16.76 percent, and transport third at 16.37 percent.
Q18-main.boundary: This is a strong finding for my framework and context, but it should still be adapted carefully in other regions.

Q19-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你有没有真正读懂自己的结果里不寻常的地方。
Q19-main.conclusion: One notable point is that old communities are not weak in every domain, and another is that health and emergency issues moved to the center of the framework.
Q19-main.logic: This means community support can partly compensate for weak infrastructure, while post-pandemic risk reshapes what counts most.
Q19-main.evidence: Wei'erlu performed weaker overall, but it still showed relative strength in support and social dimensions compared with some hard infrastructure indicators.
Q19-main.boundary: These are important patterns, but I must also keep my numbers and wording fully consistent in the viva.

Q20-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你能不能主动承认 limitation，同时不把论文说垮。
Q20-main.conclusion: The main limitation is that the framework was validated with only three communities in Henan and still depends partly on expert judgment.
Q20-main.logic: This affects external generalization more than internal framework validity.
Q20-main.evidence: The study is Henan-focused, cross-sectional, and built through expert validation and AHP weighting rather than large-scale longitudinal data.
Q20-main.boundary: So the thesis stands as a validated framework, but not as a final universal model.

Q21-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你对‘普适性’是不是讲得足够准确，不会说过头。
Q21-main.conclusion: The framework has structural generalizability, but not direct parameter generalizability.
Q21-main.logic: The method chain and logic can travel to other contexts, but the exact indicators and weights should be recalibrated locally.
Q21-main.evidence: The system is built as a full chain of extraction, validation, weighting, and testing, which can be reused in other regions.
Q21-main.boundary: So I do not claim that the Henan weights can be copied directly to other provinces or countries.

Q22-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你能不能把理论贡献和实践贡献分开说清楚。
Q22-main.conclusion: The thesis contributes both theoretically and practically.
Q22-main.logic: In theory, it shifts age-friendly evaluation toward health and resilience. In practice, it gives a structured tool for diagnosis and decision-making.
Q22-main.evidence: The top weights placed health services, emergency response, and transport at the center, and the case study proved the framework can distinguish real communities.
Q22-main.boundary: So it is not only a practical checklist, but it is also not theory without application.

Q23-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你未来研究是不是顺着论文逻辑走，而不是空泛展望。
Q23-main.conclusion: The next step is to expand the cases and test the framework in more regions and community types.
Q23-main.logic: Before building a larger platform, I should first strengthen external validity and see how stable the framework is across contexts.
Q23-main.evidence: At this stage, the framework has been tested in three communities, so the clear next step is broader validation.
Q23-main.boundary: Digital platforms and AI tools are useful, but they should come after stronger validation.

Q24-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你论文的实际用途是不是具体，而不是口号。
Q24-main.conclusion: The most realistic application is to support priority setting in community renewal and age-friendly improvement.
Q24-main.logic: A weighted framework helps decision-makers compare communities, find weak points, and allocate limited resources more clearly.
Q24-main.evidence: The three-community validation already showed that the system can separate performance levels and point to different strengths and weaknesses.
Q24-main.boundary: So the framework is ready as a prototype tool, but wider application still needs local calibration and more cases.

Q25-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你知不知道谁是真正的使用者，谁是次级使用者。
Q25-main.conclusion: The main users are local government and community managers, while planners, service providers, and researchers are also important users.
Q25-main.logic: They can all use the same framework, but at different levels and for different decisions.
Q25-main.evidence: For example, top-level domains support policy choices, while detailed indicators support diagnosis and local improvement.
Q25-main.boundary: Residents may use only a simplified version, not the full technical framework.

Q26-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你为什么要用 FDM，以及专家选择是不是有理有据。
Q26-main.conclusion: I used Fuzzy Delphi because SciBERT can generate candidates, but experts are still needed to refine, localize, and validate the final indicator set.
Q26-main.logic: The method lets me turn a large candidate pool into a more stable and agreed framework through repeated expert consensus.
Q26-main.evidence: The process used 15 interdisciplinary experts, two rounds, reduced 55 indicators to 48, and kept 100 percent expert retention.
Q26-main.boundary: So FDM is not replacing evidence. It is refining evidence into a workable framework.

Q27-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你能不能解释‘为什么 Delphi 没有老人’而不显得回避。
Q27-main.conclusion: I did not use elderly residents as Delphi experts because the Delphi stage needed stable professional judgment for framework calibration.
Q27-main.logic: Resident experience and expert calibration are not the same task, so I separated them rather than mixing them in one stage.
Q27-main.evidence: Elderly needs still entered the study through literature, policy texts, service and satisfaction indicators, and the case validation stage.
Q27-main.boundary: So I do not claim elderly people were absent from the study. I only say they were not used as Delphi experts.

Q28-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你的 AHP 实施过程是不是严谨、是不是过了一致性检验。
Q28-main.conclusion: I implemented AHP on the final validated hierarchy and controlled credibility through consistency checks.
Q28-main.logic: Experts compared indicators in pairs, their matrices were checked, and only consistent judgments were used to form the group weights.
Q28-main.evidence: All expert consistency ratios were below 0.1, and the average CR was 0.0567.
Q28-main.boundary: AHP still involves expert judgment, but here the judgment is transparent, testable, and logically checked.

Q29-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你为什么最后没有用机器学习赋权，以及你是否诚实面对这个限制。
Q29-main.conclusion: I used AHP because, under my current sample size and data conditions, it was more defensible and more stable than machine-learning-based weighting.
Q29-main.logic: Machine learning needs many more high-quality community cases, while my thesis had a small number of validated case communities.
Q29-main.evidence: At this stage I had only three case-study communities, which is far from the scale needed for stable model training.
Q29-main.boundary: So this is also one of my main limitations, and a larger future dataset may support a different method later.

Q30-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你案例测量是不是尽量客观、可重复、可解释。
Q30-main.conclusion: I measured the three communities through one standard protocol and as much objective evidence as possible.
Q30-main.logic: I combined field observation, direct measurement, office data, records, and map-based information, then standardized the results into one scoring framework.
Q30-main.evidence: These data were entered into the 48-indicator weighted system and produced the final scores of 85.27, 71.24, and 54.50.
Q30-main.boundary: A small amount of judgment is still involved, but the protocol was designed to keep it controlled and repeatable.

Q31-main.purposeZh: 老师想看你前端筛选和数据清洗是不是透明，能不能说清它在方法链里的位置。
Q31-main.conclusion: PRISMA and data cleaning are the quality-control bridge of the whole method chain.
Q31-main.logic: PRISMA controls the text input side, and data cleaning controls the candidate indicator side before expert validation starts.
Q31-main.evidence: They helped narrow the process from a broad search to 410 qualified texts, then from 847 candidates to 55 high-quality indicators.
Q31-main.boundary: So these steps are not minor technical work. They are a key reason why the later validation became more stable.
